name: Vía Augusta and Laguna de los Tollos Loop
name_original: Lagona de los Tollos — circular desde El Cuervo de Sevilla
description: Hike the Vía Augusta and Laguna de los Tollos Loop, a moderate 6.0-mile trail through a Special Area of Conservation with rich birdlife and
region_intro: Hiking around El Cuervo De Sevilla offers a diverse landscape at the southern tip of Seville province, bordering Cádiz. The region serves as a transitional area between the agricultural plains of La Campiña and the significant wetlands of Las Marismas. Key natural features include the Laguna de Los Tollos, a protected wetland and the third-largest lagoon in Andalusia, and areas of gentle hill country. These varied environments provide a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around El Cuervo De Sevilla The most popular hiking route is Vía Augusta and Laguna de los Tollos Loop, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines historical Roman paths with views of the ecologically significant Laguna de los Tollos. Another top favourite among local hikers is Balsa de Melendo Trail, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.4 km) path. This route explores the local terrain, offering views of the surrounding agricultural and natural areas. Local hikers also love the Birdwatching Platform loop from Laguna de los Tollos, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through the wetland ecosystem, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Hiking around El Cuervo De Sevilla is defined by wetland ecosystems, agricultural plains, and gentle hill country. The network offers options for both easy walks and more moderate hiking trails. The routes in El Cuervo De Sevilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 70 hikers have used komoot to explore El Cuervo De Sevilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Cuervo De Sevilla?
A: El Cuervo De Sevilla offers a good selection of routes, with over 20 hiking trails to explore. These range from easy strolls to more moderate hikes, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in El Cuervo De Sevilla?
A: The trails in El Cuervo De Sevilla are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique wetland scenery and the opportunity for birdwatching, particularly around the Laguna de Los Tollos.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in El Cuervo De Sevilla?
A: Hiking in El Cuervo De Sevilla offers a diverse experience, transitioning between the agricultural plains of La Campiña and the significant wetlands of Las Marismas. You'll encounter the ecologically important Laguna de Los Tollos, gentle hill country, and open fields, providing a unique blend of natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in El Cuervo De Sevilla?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14586672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lebrija – Parking Lot in Lebrija loop from Lebrija&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.5 km circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14586636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parking Lot in Lebrija loop from Lebrija&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 10.7 km.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, El Cuervo De Sevilla has several easy trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40385573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching Platform loop from Laguna de los Tollos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.0 km route, perfect for a relaxed walk and observing local wildlife. This area is generally suitable for families looking for gentle outdoor activities.

Q: What are the best places for birdwatching while hiking?
A: The best place for birdwatching is undoubtedly the &lt;strong&gt;Laguna de Los Tollos&lt;/strong&gt;. This protected wetland is the third-largest lagoon in Andalusia and is vital for diverse birdlife, including threatened species like the red-knobbed coot and ruddy shelduck. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40385573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching Platform loop from Laguna de los Tollos&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed for this purpose.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the region has historical significance. Parts of the ancient Roman road, the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Augusta&lt;/strong&gt;, can be explored, offering a historical dimension to your hike. You can experience this on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009027395/via-augusta-and-laguna-de-los-tollos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Augusta and Laguna de los Tollos Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6169555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Fontanal Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the hiking trails?
A: Many trails, especially those around the Laguna de Los Tollos, can be accessed from the &lt;strong&gt;Parque Rocío de la Cámara&lt;/strong&gt;. This municipal park often serves as a convenient starting point and likely offers parking facilities. For routes near Lebrija, look for designated parking areas in the town.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: While specific regulations for each trail can vary, many natural areas in Spain are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. However, for protected areas like Laguna de Los Tollos, it's always best to check local signage or official park guidelines to ensure compliance and protect the sensitive wildlife.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay in El Cuervo De Sevilla?
A: El Cuervo De Sevilla is a town with local amenities. You can find cafes, pubs, and restaurants within the town itself. For accommodation, there are options available in El Cuervo De Sevilla or the nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5389253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lebrija&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a starting point for some trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in El Cuervo De Sevilla?
A: The best time to hike in El Cuervo De Sevilla is generally during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May), to avoid the intense summer heat. These seasons also offer excellent conditions for birdwatching at Laguna de Los Tollos, particularly during migration periods.
tips: El Cuervo de Sevilla is a locality in the province of Seville, Andalusia, Spain. It is located right on the border with the province of Cádiz.

Park that is close to La Laguna de los Tollos and where there is a large space with trees and green areas where you can spend the day or rest. At the end of the park, the Ermita de Nuestra Señora del Rosario appears, a chapel with the characteristic colors of the area: white and yellow.

faqs.question: Where does the Vía Augusta and Laguna de los Tollos Loop trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts from El Cuervo de Sevilla (Parish of San José). There is usually parking available near the starting points in El Cuervo, especially around the Laguna de los Tollos area, which often has a dedicated parking lot for visitors.

faqs.question: How difficult is this loop, and is it suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While it requires a good level of fitness, its relatively flat terrain with around 50 meters of elevation gain makes it generally manageable for various skill levels, including beginners with some hiking experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain around Laguna de los Tollos is primarily a dirt path, described as easily accessible. Sections of the broader Vía Augusta in this region are generally flat, with some low hills, and can include quiet tracks, sandy sections, and occasional tarmac or rural roads.

faqs.question: What natural features and wildlife can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The main attraction is the Laguna de los Tollos, a significant wetland and a vital habitat for numerous bird species. You can expect to see waders like the Black-winged Stilt and European Golden Plover, as well as threatened species such as the Marbled Duck and Red-knobbed Coot. Flamingos also use the lagoon as a resting and transit area. There are viewpoints and wooden benches along the path for birdwatching.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks on the loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail passes through El Cuervo de Sevilla (Parish of San José), which is a common starting point. You'll also encounter the Rocio de la Cámara Park and Camino de Santiago, which is part of the historical pilgrimage route.

faqs.question: Does the loop connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it follows parts of the Vía Augusta de Cádiz a Sevilla, Camino de las Monjas, Paseo de Amalia Gómez Gómez, and the Vía Augusta desde Cádiz /Jérez de la Frontera a El Cuero.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is generally during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May), to avoid the intense summer heat. This period also offers excellent opportunities for birdwatching, especially during migration seasons.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Laguna de los Tollos is a Special Area of Conservation, dogs are generally permitted on trails as long as they are kept on a leash to protect the local wildlife and natural environment. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Laguna de los Tollos Special Area of Conservation?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Laguna de los Tollos Special Area of Conservation. It is freely accessible to the public, though visitors are expected to respect the natural environment and any posted regulations.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Starting from El Cuervo de Sevilla (Parish of San José), you'll find various amenities in the town, including cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can stock up on supplies before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this loop?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, bring comfortable walking shoes, water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and binoculars for birdwatching. A light snack is also a good idea. Dress in layers, especially during cooler months.
